description_long: This object is a patch of sandy terrain with a slightly raised, uneven surface and a visible depression or trench running diagonally across it. The sand is a medium brown color with scattered lighter speckles, suggesting a mix of fine and coarse grains. The edges of the patch are irregular, with one side featuring a distinct drop-off or cut, possibly indicating erosion or excavation. The surface texture appears granular and matte, with no visible vegetation or debris. The depression in the sand patch creates a shadowed area, emphasizing the change in elevation. This type of sand patch could be found in urban parks, construction sites, or roadside areas where sand has accumulated or been deposited.
description: A medium brown, granular sand patch with a diagonal depression and irregular edges, featuring a matte, speckled surface. The patch has a visible drop-off and shadowed trench, suggesting uneven terrain.
description_view_0: From this angle, the sand patch's depression and raised edge are clearly visible, with the shadowed trench running from left to right.
description_view_1: This view highlights the depth and length of the diagonal trench, showing the irregular cut and the way the sand slopes downward.
description_view_2: The depression appears deepest from this perspective, with the sand patch's irregular boundary and the shadowed area most prominent.
description_view_3: Here, the raised edge and the start of the depression are visible, with the sand patch's surface texture and speckling clearly shown.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside, or construction site
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and somewhat well-defined, with visible edges and a distinct drop-off on one side.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: The sand patch appears partially blended with its surroundings, with some edges more abrupt and others gradually transitioning.
